> **⚠ Do not merge yet.** The RunB dispatch implementation is complete and assembles cleanly, but `runb2` has not been tested on real hardware or an emulator. Functional validation of the new RunB variant must happen before this is merged.

## Goal

Build the RunB module directly from `basic09.asm` rather than maintaining it as a separate source file (`runb.asm`). Having a single source for both BASIC09 and RunB eliminates divergence between the two and ensures any runtime fixes apply to both automatically.

## Summary

- Introduces the Microware `INCLUDED` bitmask (`EDITOR=$01`, `RUNTIM=$02`, `MATHPAK=$04`) into `basic09.asm`, mirroring the original Microware `b09type` build-time flag
- Wraps EDITOR-only sections (keyword/token scan tables, and the full EDITOR command infrastructure) in `ifne INCLUDED&EDITOR` guards so they are excluded when building RunB
- Implements all 9 RunB dispatch functions (`SETEXT`, `EXIT`, `BYEBYE`, `KILLEX`, `KILALL`, `DIRLNK`, `L1287`, `L18BE`, `L1BA2`) and supporting helpers in an `ifeq INCLUDED&EDITOR` block in the pre-`start:` region of `basic09.asm`, ported from `runb.asm`
- Building with `-DINCLUDED=RUNTIM+MATHPAK` now produces a working `RunB` module from `basic09.asm`; without the flag it defaults to the full `FULL` bitmask and produces an identical BASIC09 binary (CRC verified)
- Adds `runb2_6809` and `runb2_6309` targets to the makefile and includes them in the default build and disk image alongside the traditional `runb_6809`/`runb_6309` built from `runb.asm`

## Size difference between runb2 and runb

`runb2_6809` (11828 bytes) is 371 bytes smaller than `runb_6809` (12199 bytes). This is expected and structural, not a bug:

| Component | `runb_6809` (from `runb.asm`) | `runb2_6809` (from `basic09.asm`) |
|-----------|-------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| Pre-`start:` block (exec offset) | 405 bytes ($0195) | 743 bytes ($02E7) |
| RunB-specific post-`start:` helpers | ~709 bytes | ~12 bytes |
| Shared RUNTIM code | identical | identical |
| **Total** | **12199 bytes** | **11828 bytes** |

`runb.asm` was written as a self-contained executable and carries ~709 bytes of standalone helpers after `start:` — its own name parser (`LAX1`, `LAX2`, `SkipSpac`, `IsAlpha`), a standalone `PrintErr`, parameter decode (`L04FF`), and a private `$1E` dispatch table (`L0468`) routing to those functions.

`basic09.asm`'s RunB mode doesn't need any of that: the `L000D` jump-vector table maps `u001E` → `L1CA5` unconditionally (same in both BASIC09 and RunB modes), so dispatch functions like `KILLEX` and `DIRLNK` route through the shared `CMPRAM`/`NAMSYM`/`SEARC0`/`MOVDWN` infrastructure already present in the RUNTIM section. The pre-`start:` RunB dispatch block (our 338-byte addition) covers everything runb.asm's standalone helpers provided.

Net: runb.asm is larger by 709 − 338 = **371 bytes**, confirmed by the map files — shared RUNTIM symbols (`cnty`, `Y19`, `eom`) sit at addresses exactly $0173 = 371 bytes higher in `runb_6809` than in `runb2_6809`.

## Test plan

- [x] `make basic09_6809` — CRC matches baseline (`$CCB2BE`)
- [x] `make basic09_6309` — CRC matches baseline (`$DC426B`)
- [x] `make runb2_6809 runb2_6309` — both assemble without errors, valid CRC, Edition 22
- [x] `make all` — all six objects build cleanly
- [x] `make dsk` — disk image created with all objects
- [ ] Run `runb2_6809` on hardware or emulator and validate basic program execution
- [ ] Run `runb2_6309` on hardware or emulator and validate basic program execution

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
